hard.next_step: 20% 2nd July - work with TCS and use their muscle. RFP expected by end of July 23rd June 26 - Met Sharon Ooi (Head vendor contracts and compliance) and Soon Hin (VP and Head - Regional sourcing, digitization and Data Analytics). RFP will be released in Aug 26, currently undergoing procurement transformation excerise - by TCS. completion by end of July.
hard.ais_why: RHB Banking Group (Malaysia) – Critical AIS Assessment. Enterprise AI maturity 9.5 (very mature AI agenda across banking operations); Procurement transformation maturity 7.2 (limited public evidence of procurement modernization); Procurement complexity 8.8 (large indirect spend across technology, facilities, consulting, outsourcing, marketing, professional services); Executive willingness to invest 8.2; Zycus competitive fit 8.0 (good fit, though banks often have entrenched ERP/procurement ecosystems and lengthy governance).

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage BFSI deal cooling 13 days after the 23 June discovery: a TCS-run transformation exercise finishing end-July gates an August RFP, so the window to shape criteria is closing, yet there is no champion, no economic buyer, and no buyer reply since the 2 July rep outreach.
north_star_verdict.math: 147 days to close (30 Nov 2026). Created 23 Jun, moved to Qualified 24 Jun, ~13 days in Qualified as of 6 Jul. Amount raised 150k to 250k on 24 Jun (scope up, not down). Next Step (2 Jul) puts RFP release in August and TCS transformation completion end-July, so the pre-RFP influence window is ~2-4 weeks. Post-RFP the remaining path (evaluation, shortlist, commercials, InfoSec, legal, contracting) must compress into ~120 days to hold the 30 Nov date, which is aggressive for a 9-product enterprise S2P suite in a bank. No forward slip logged yet, but the TCS gate and single-thread engagement make the date fragile.
evidence: Last verified buyer engagement: 23 Jun discovery meeting with Sharon Ooi (Head, Vendor Contracts and Compliance) and Ooi Soon Hin (Next_Step__c)
Rep outreach 2 Jul (Next Step update) awaiting buyer reply; no confirmed two-way touch since 23 Jun
Decision Maker identified (Ooi Soon Hin, PRIMARY contact role); Economic Buyer unmapped
TCS procurement transformation exercise (completion end-July per Next_Step__c 2 Jul) gates the August RFP and is the critical path
No champion and no multi-thread beyond one contact role plus one Next-Step-named influencer (Sharon Ooi)
Full S2P suite in scope (9 products incl. Merlin Intake and Agentic AI, Products__c) and strong AIS 8.3, but no quantified pain or ROI case on record
Amount raised from 150k to 250k on 24 Jun (OpportunityFieldHistory), scope expanding not contracting

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists yet. The 23 June discovery with Ooi Soon Hin and Sharon Ooi (Next_Step__c) captured no cost-savings target, cycle-time, error-rate, headcount, or ROI figure, and there are no Avoma calls on record to add buyer numbers. The pain is real but qualitative: RHB is mid-way through a TCS-run procurement transformation (Next_Step__c 2 Jul) and has a broad 9-product S2P scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, Products__c), which implies material indirect-spend leakage (AIS notes large indirect spend across technology, facilities, consulting, outsourcing, marketing, and professional services). Because the 250k business case is un-quantified, the ROI story for the eventual economic-buyer approval will be generic. The move is a working session with Ooi Soon Hin to quantify current-state pain (sourcing cycle time, contract redline effort, supplier-onboarding delay, invoice error rate, compliance exposure) and align it to the TCS findings before the August RFP.
sources: Next_Step__c (23 Jun discovery, 2 Jul TCS note)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. The only contact role is Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker, PRIMARY), a procurement-line head who owns sourcing and digitalization but is unlikely to be the sole budget authority for a 250k enterprise platform in a bank with lengthy governance (AIS: 'lengthy governance processes'). No OpportunityContactRole, MEDDPICC field, or call names a CFO, CIO, or Group CPO, and there are no Avoma transcripts to infer budget control from, so an inference would be invented rather than evidence-based. What the evidence does show is that commercial authority sits above the current thread and that TCS is shaping the process, so the budget decision may be influenced by the TCS target-operating-model recommendation. The move is to ask Ooi Soon Hin who signs off the transformation budget and to secure an introduction to that executive during the evaluation phase.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Ooi Soon Hin, Decision Maker, PRIMARY)

decision_criteria.narrative: The formal evaluation criteria are not yet known because they are being authored inside the TCS procurement transformation exercise and will land in the August RFP (Next_Step__c 2 Jul: 'RFP will be released in Aug 26, currently undergoing procurement transformation exercise - by TCS'). No use-case review, ShoeFit/BRD session, or demo has taken place, so nothing is confirmed. What is inferable from the 9-product scope and BFSI context is that criteria will weight compliance, risk controls, data governance, and AI capability heavily (AIS rates enterprise AI maturity 9.5 and procurement complexity 8.8). The single highest-leverage move is to get sight of, or influence, the TCS-drafted RFP requirements before release so Zycus can map its Merlin/Agentic AI differentiation into the scoring criteria.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is a TCS-mediated, RFP-driven enterprise evaluation: RHB is running a procurement transformation with TCS that completes end-July, then releases an RFP in August (Next_Step__c 2 Jul). This tells us the process is structured and consultant-led rather than a direct sole-source, and that TCS is a gatekeeper whose recommendations will shape the shortlist. The buying committee is only partially visible: Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker, procurement) and Sharon Ooi (Vendor Contracts and Compliance) surfaced at the 23 June discovery, but the approval chain, IT/security stakeholders, and the sign-off authority are unmapped. The move is to map the RFP governance (who evaluates, who scores, who approves) and to build a direct line to the TCS transformation leads before the August release.
sources: Next_Step__c 2 Jul (TCS completion end-July, RFP August)

paper_process.narrative: The contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) are unknown at this Qualified stage. The one positive signal is that Sharon Ooi, Head of Vendor Contracts and Compliance, was already in the 23 June discovery (Next_Step__c), so the legal/contracting owner is identified early, which is useful for a bank where InfoSec and vendor-onboarding governance are heavy. The iSaaS delivery model plus BFSI regulation will require SOC evidence, a security questionnaire, and data-residency/governance review (AIS flags entrenched ecosystems and lengthy governance). Nothing about MSA paper, redline timeline, or PO process is confirmed. Visibility will come from asking Sharon Ooi to walk Zycus through RHB's vendor-onboarding, InfoSec, and contracting steps as part of the RFP.
sources: Next_Step__c 23 Jun (Sharon Ooi, Vendor Contracts and Compliance)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain and a change mandate are confirmed but not quantified. RHB is 'currently undergoing procurement transformation exercise - by TCS' (Next_Step__c 2 Jul), which is an explicit signal of dissatisfaction with the current procurement operating model and executive appetite to change. The 9-product S2P scope (Products__c) implies pain across contracting, sourcing, supplier management, P2P, invoicing, risk, and intake automation, and the AIS assessment points to large fragmented indirect spend (complexity 8.8). The pain owner is Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker, Regional Sourcing and Digitalization, Group Procurement). What is missing is the specific, quantified articulation (manual effort, cycle time, leakage, compliance risk) directly from the buyer. The move is to surface and quantify current-state pain with Ooi Soon Hin and align it to the TCS transformation findings before the RFP.
sources: Next_Step__c 2 Jul ('procurement transformation exercise - by TCS')

champion.narrative: No champion has emerged. The only mapped stakeholder is Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker), who is the process owner but has shown no advocacy behaviour, no internal selling, no shared documents, and no proactive engagement beyond the 23 June discovery (Next_Step__c). Sharon Ooi (Vendor Contracts and Compliance) also attended that meeting but is not a contact role and has demonstrated no advocacy. The nine 'Opportunity Health' call-attempt tasks logged 24 Jun ('Decision Maker is not identified', 'Keep the engagement multi-threaded', 'Prospect's gone cold? No inbound since last 14 days') confirm the deal team itself flags a single-thread, cooling relationship. In a TCS-mediated RFP this is high-risk: with no insider advocate, Zycus has no early read on the RFP criteria and no influence if the committee defers to TCS. The move is to convert Ooi Soon Hin into a coach (early RFP feedback, an intro to the TCS leads) and to multi-thread into IT/security and the budget owner.
sources: SF Tasks 24 Jun (Opportunity Health: DM/champion/multi-thread flags)

competition.narrative: No competitor is named anywhere on record: the Competitors field is blank, and no rival appears in the 23 June discovery or the 2 July Next Step, and there are no Avoma calls to surface a shortlist. Critically, this is NOT a sole-source: RHB is running a TCS-led transformation feeding an August RFP (Next_Step__c 2 Jul), so a multi-vendor field is coming, and the real risk is that TCS's target-operating-model work may already carry an incumbent or preferred-vendor lean that Zycus cannot yet see. The blind spot is that competition will be defined by others (TCS + the RFP) before Zycus has any intelligence on it. The move is to ask Ooi Soon Hin directly who else is being considered and whether TCS holds a preferred-vendor or incumbent relationship, and to get in front of TCS before the RFP locks the field. Per living memory, no competitor is asserted since none is evidenced.
sources: Next_Step__c 23 Jun and 2 Jul (no competitor named; TCS-led RFP)

deal_movement.summary: The deal advanced structurally in late June then cooled: qualified 24 Jun with the value raised to 250k, one discovery meeting held 23 Jun, and a 2 Jul rep-side Next Step logged the TCS/RFP timeline, but no confirmed buyer reply has followed and the last two-way touch remains 23 Jun.

items.change: Moved to Qualified from Initial Interest
Owner set to George John; Type set to New Business; Division moved to New Markets
Discovery meeting with Ooi Soon Hin and Sharon Ooi
Rep Next Step logged: work with TCS, RFP expected August, TCS transformation completes end-July

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has surfaced on any source (Competitors__c blank; nothing named in the 23 Jun discovery or 2 Jul Next Step; no Avoma calls), but this is not a sole-source win: a TCS-run transformation completing end-July feeds an August RFP, so a multi-vendor field is imminent and the strongest current threat is that TCS defines the shortlist and criteria before Zycus has any competitive intelligence. The task now is to discover the field and TCS's leanings before the RFP, not to assume a named rival.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is inferred, not yet validated on a call. RHB's mature AI agenda (AIS enterprise-AI maturity 9.5) aligns well with Zycus's Merlin Intake and Agentic AI scope, but BFSI governance, security, and data-residency expectations are unconfirmed and will be tested in the August RFP.

No ROI, savings, or cycle-time figure captured from the 23 Jun discovery

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage BFSI RFP deal with a closing pre-RFP influence window: the win levers are getting inside the TCS-drafted criteria, building a coach, and multi-threading to power before August.
flags: Get in front of the TCS transformation leads before end-July so Zycus can influence the RFP criteria rather than react to them.
Convert Ooi Soon Hin from a passive Decision Maker into a coach who shares early RFP feedback and names the budget owner.
Multi-thread beyond a single procurement contact into IT/security and the economic buyer before the RFP locks the committee.

items.title: Head, System Admin, Regional Sourcing and Digitalization & Data Analytics – Group Procurement
items.risk: Single-thread and cooling: attended the 23 Jun discovery but no confirmed reply to the 2 Jul rep outreach. Owns the RFP process but may defer to TCS recommendations without a Zycus advocate.

items.name: Sharon Ooi
items.title: Head, Vendor Contracts and Compliance
items.risk: Legal/compliance owner named in the 23 Jun discovery but not a contact role and with no follow-up engagement; contracting and InfoSec path through her is unmapped.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker) is the RFP process owner but has shown no advocacy or proactive engagement beyond the 23 June discovery, and the deal team's own 24 Jun Opportunity Health tasks flag a single-thread, cooling relationship with no inbound in 14 days. In a TCS-mediated RFP this leaves Zycus with no insider intelligence or influence path before the August release.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole, Next_Step__c (23 Jun, 2 Jul), SF Tasks 24 Jun

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's AI story (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI in scope) is a strong match to RHB's mature AI agenda (AIS 8.3, enterprise AI maturity 9.5), but it has not yet been landed with the buyer on a call, so the differentiation is potential rather than proven.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.3 / Strong, driven by a very mature enterprise AI agenda (9.5) and high procurement complexity (8.8); no call yet exists to confirm whether the AI appetite is under- or over-rated versus the buyer's stated priorities (customer experience, risk, compliance, efficiency).

items.detail: TCS controls the transformation exercise and RFP criteria; if TCS holds an incumbent or preferred-vendor lean, Zycus is exposed with no visibility.
No economic buyer mapped and only one procurement-line contact; a 250k platform in a bank needs executive sign-off that is not yet accessible.
August RFP plus a full 9-product enterprise evaluation, InfoSec, and contracting must compress into ~120 days to hold the 30 Nov close; date is optimistic.
BFSI InfoSec, data-residency, and vendor-onboarding governance are heavy and unstarted; iSaaS delivery will require SOC evidence and a security questionnaire.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is thinning: one discovery meeting on 23 Jun, a rep-side Next Step on 2 Jul awaiting reply, and 24 Jun Opportunity Health tasks explicitly flagging 'no inbound in 14 days'. Not cold, but single-thread and cooling.

critical_signals.text: No rival has surfaced yet, but a TCS-run transformation finishing end-July will define an August RFP field, so competition will be set by others before Zycus has any intelligence on it.

The 23 June discovery with Ooi Soon Hin and Sharon Ooi produced a change mandate but no quantified pain, no competitor read, and no committed next meeting, and the buyer has not replied to the 2 July follow-up.
Sharon Ooi, Head of Vendor Contracts and Compliance, appeared at discovery, an early handle on the legal and InfoSec path, but the team has no follow-up engagement with her.
The opportunity was raised from 150k to 250k on 24 June as scope grew to a nine-product suite, but there is still no ROI or savings figure to justify the number.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer preference expressed; only one discovery meeting and no competitive comparison on record.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative change mandate confirmed ('currently undergoing procurement transformation exercise - by TCS', Next_Step__c 2 Jul); no quantified ROI, savings, or cycle-time figures.

scope_change.detail: Amount raised from 150k to 250k on 24 Jun (OpportunityFieldHistory) as the nine-product S2P scope firmed up; scope is expanding, not contracting.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early-stage BFSI deal with a strong AI fit (AIS 8.3) but no champion, no economic buyer, and no competitive read, racing a closing pre-RFP window before a TCS-led August RFP; the one thing between us and a strong position is getting inside the TCS criteria and mapping power before release.

win_position.text: This sits as an early, unqualified-for-power position: only Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker) is mapped, no economic buyer or champion exists, and the competitive field is unknown 13 days after the 23 June discovery.
The upside is a genuine change mandate plus a top-tier AI fit: RHB is mid-transformation with TCS and AIS rates enterprise AI maturity 9.5 against Zycus's Merlin Intake and Agentic AI scope.
The biggest risk is that TCS defines the August RFP criteria and shortlist before Zycus has any influence or a coach on the inside (Next_Step__c 2 Jul).
The 250k value was raised on 24 June with no quantified ROI behind it, so the commercial case is currently generic.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is thinning: one discovery meeting on 23 June, a rep-side Next Step on 2 July with no buyer reply, and a 24 June task flagging no inbound in 14 days.
There is a concrete clock the buyer set: TCS transformation completes end-July and the RFP releases in August (Next_Step__c 2 Jul).
The buyer has not requested the next meeting and no close plan has concretised, so the cadence is rep-pushed rather than buyer-pulled.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is low: no champion, a single procurement contact, and no proactive buyer engagement beyond attending the 23 June discovery.
The buyer has committed to a formal RFP that includes the full S2P scope, signalling a real intent to buy a platform rather than a point tool.

deal_risk.text: No economic buyer is accessible for a 250k bank platform, and approval will require executive sign-off that is not yet mapped.
The 30 November close is optimistic: an August RFP plus a nine-product evaluation, BFSI InfoSec, and contracting must compress into roughly 120 days.
TCS as gatekeeper is a political risk if it carries an incumbent or preferred-vendor lean Zycus cannot yet see.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; single contact role; 24 Jun Opportunity Health tasks flag DM-not-identified and no inbound in 14 days.

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB mapped; only Ooi Soon Hin (Decision Maker) on record for a 250k platform.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer preference or competitive comparison expressed on any call or field.

gaps: No Avoma calls found for this opportunity via opp, account, or attendee-email discovery; all buyer evidence is from Salesforce fields (Next_Step__c, contact role, tasks, AIS, Products).
Economic buyer, competitive field, decision criteria, and quantified business case are genuinely unknown and lower confidence.
Only one contact role on record; committee and approval chain unmapped.
